statutoryBodies.name: School Development Board (per school)
statutoryBodies.statusNote: Section 2 mandates a School Development Board for every single school in Sri Lanka. Each Board is constituted under Section 3 with a mix of ex-officio school staff and elected/nominated community representatives. The Principal serves as Chairman.
powers: Advise the Principal on school development and curriculum improvements (Section 8)
Assess current needs and performance of the school (Section 8)
Promote extracurricular activities — sports, literature, aesthetics (Section 8)
Foster collaboration between parents, teachers, and students (Section 8)
Assist in maintenance and development of school buildings, libraries, and equipment (Section 8)
Manage distribution of school books, mid-day meal stamps, and student uniforms (Section 8)
Foster community financial and material contributions (Section 8)
Form specialized sub-committees for specific tasks (Section 9)
dataGaps: Total number of School Development Boards currently constituted across Sri Lanka
Compliance rate — how many schools have active, functional Boards
Ministerial regulations issued under Section 13 — content and current status
Typical Board meeting frequency and attendance rates
Average size of School Development Funds across different school categories

statutoryBodies.name: School Development Fund (per school)
statutoryBodies.statusNote: Section 10 mandates every Board to establish and maintain a School Development Fund. The Fund finances Board activities and collects community contributions and donations. A Treasurer is appointed under Section 11 to manage the Fund.

exOfficio.note: Appointed to manage the Fund and ensure transparent accounting (Section 11)

powers: Finance Board activities and school development initiatives (Section 10)
dataGaps: Aggregate financial data across all School Development Funds
Whether Funds are subject to provincial or national audit oversight

timeline.details: Act No. 8 of 1993 commenced on February 26, 1993. Mandated the creation of a School Development Board and School Development Fund for every school in Sri Lanka, formalizing parental and community involvement in school management.

tiers.name: Minister of Education
tiers.relationship: Power to make regulations under Section 13; power to dissolve and reconstitute Boards (Section 12)

tiers.name: Provincial Councils
tiers.relationship: Education is a devolved subject — Provincial Councils oversee school administration at provincial level

tiers.name: School Development Board
tiers.relationship: Advisory and supportive body for each school, chaired by the Principal (Sections 2-9)

tiers.name: School Development Fund
tiers.relationship: Financial mechanism for each Board, managed by a Treasurer (Sections 10-11)

currentReplacement.institutional: School Development Boards (per-school advisory and development)
